Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Interface IMemoryAccessNotifier

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Provides memory access notifications for debugging and monitoring purposes.

public interface IMemoryAccessNotifier

Remarks

This interface is designed for components that need to monitor memory access, such as debuggers, memory viewers, and watchpoint systems. It provides events that fire when memory is read from or written to, allowing external tools to track program behavior, detect specific memory accesses, and implement breakpoint and watchpoint functionality.

The interface provides two notification events:

  • MemoryRead: Fired when a byte is read from memory
  • MemoryWritten: Fired when a byte is written to memory
Both events fire for individual byte operations, allowing precise tracking of memory access patterns during program execution.

Events

MemoryRead

Raised when a byte is read from memory.

event EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs> MemoryRead

Event Type

EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs>

Remarks

This event fires whenever a byte of memory is accessed through CPU operations or direct memory reads. The MemoryAccessEventArgs contains the address read from and the value at that address.

Typical uses include:

  • Implementing memory read watchpoints in a debugger
  • Tracking which memory locations a program accesses
  • Detecting access to memory-mapped I/O registers
  • Profiling memory access patterns for optimization analysis

Performance Note: This event fires even more frequently than MemoryWritten since programs typically read memory more often than they write it. Subscribers should be extremely efficient to avoid significantly impacting emulation performance.

MemoryWritten

Raised when a byte is written to memory.

event EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs> MemoryWritten

Event Type

EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs>

Remarks

This event fires whenever a byte of memory is modified through CPU operations or direct memory writes. The MemoryAccessEventArgs contains the address written to and the new value at that address.

Typical uses include:

  • Implementing memory write watchpoints in a debugger
  • Tracking changes to specific memory locations
  • Logging memory write patterns for analysis
  • Triggering breakpoints when specific values are written to specific addresses

Performance Note: This event can fire very frequently during program execution (potentially millions of times per second). Subscribers should minimize processing time to avoid impacting emulation performance.
